text: The surgeon uses an Alexis wound retractor to safely reduce the exteriorized bowel through a small midline laparotomy incision, which extends from the umbilical port site. The retractor is placed to protect the wound and provide retraction after colonic mobilization and ileocolic pedicle ligation.
The surgeon returns the resected bowel to the abdomen by unfolding the white ring of the Alexis wound retractor, allowing the bowel to pass through the Alexis tunnel back into the abdominal cavity.

The surgeon twists the alexis wound retractor to create a seal, then applies gentle pressure to the bowel, allowing it to slide smoothly through the retractor's tunnel. The alexis is then untwisted, and the green ring is removed from the abdomen.
The surgeon closes the wound in a standard manner after the bowel has been returned to the abdomen.
